title: Marlierea edulis
intro: Marlierea edulis is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the "Cipo" or "Cipo fruit". The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Marlierea edulis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as tinctures and ointments.
